title: pultenaea elachista
intro: Pultenaea elachista, also known as the Golden Pea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to eastern Australia and is often found growing in dry forests and woodlands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, coughs, and skin conditions. The Golden Pea can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and has also been used in traditional medicine preparations, such as tinctures and salves, to promote healing and well-being.
